User question:
how to perform wall sits?

Coach answer:
completion: you can perform this exercise anywhere you have access to a flat wall. start with your back against a wall with your feet shoulder width and about 2 feet from the wall. engage your abdominal muscles and slowly slide your back down the wall until your thighs are parallel to the ground. adjust your feet so your knees are directly above your ankles. keep your back flat against the wall. hold the position for 20 to 60 seconds.    slide slowly back up the wall to a standing position. rest for 30 seconds and repeat the exercise three times. increase your hold time in five-second increments as you increase your strength.

what are the different types of stretches to improve flexibility?

User question:
how to do a lat pulldown?

completion: to perform a lat pulldown correctly, begin by sitting on the pulldown machine bench, feet flat on the floor, and knees snug under the kneepad. grasp the bar overhand with hands shoulder-width apart, then lean back slightly while keeping a neutral spine supported by abdominal muscles. inhale as you pull the bar down to the chest by engaging your elbows and squeezing your shoulder blades together. how much cardio should i be doing?

Coach answer:

how to perform standing calf raises?

Coach answer:
completion: stand on the floor with your feet shoulder-width apart, toes pointing forward. keep your back straight, shoulders back and down, and abs pulled in. raise your heels slowly, keeping your knees extended but not locked. pause for one second when you are standing as much on the tips of your toes as you can. lower your heels back to the ground, returning to the starting position. it does not require any equipment so it is an easy exercise to perform at home, at the gym, and when traveling.

what are the 5 components of fitness?
